name: Frame interpolation (FILM) — smoother or slow-motion video
description:
Make a video smoother — or turn it into slow motion. FILM generates in-between frames, multiplying the frame count by your chosen factor (2× = twice as many frames).

• **Keep real-time speed** checked → the clip plays at the same speed but looks smoother (higher frame rate).
• Unchecked → the extra frames stretch the clip into **slow motion** at the original frame rate.

The original audio is carried through — best with 'keep real-time speed' on; in slow-motion mode the audio won't match the longer video.
author: Template - ComfyUI
